slides.title: Family, Identity, and History
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 7 English, Unit 1: Family, Identity, and History. Welcome to Year 7 English at Ehel Academy! This is Unit 1: Family, Identity, and History. In this unit, you will explore some of the most important questions a person can ask: Where do I come from? Who are the people in my family? What stories have been passed down through the generations? You will read about families, learn how to describe your own background, and discover how people across East Africa and the wider world have moved, settled, and built new lives over the centuries. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand a passage about family history and migration, and discuss its ideas in English
Use the past simple tense correctly to describe completed actions and events
Distinguish between the past simple and the present perfect tense
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand a passage about family history and migration, and discuss its ideas in english. you will also use the past simple tense correctly to describe completed actions and events. you will also distinguish between the past simple and the present perfect tense. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: belligerent, malevolence, vindictive, infuriate, animosity, narcissistic. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Family, Identity, and History: source text 1
Family, Identity, and History: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Family, Identity, and History: source text 1 and Family, Identity, and History: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: The Past Simple Tense
bullets: What it means and why it matters The past simple tense is used to talk about actions or events that happened and finished in the past. It is the most...
slides.narration: The first language focus is The Past Simple Tense. What it means and why it matters The past simple tense is used to talk about actions or events that happened and finished in the past. It is the most important tense for telling stories, describing experiences, and talking about history. When we say "I played football yesterday" or "She went to the market last week," we are using the past simple. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
